metadata.title: Daśamahāvidyā Kavacam
metadata.subtitle: The Armor of the Ten Great Wisdom Goddesses

verses.sanskrit:
ॐ प्राच्यां रक्षतु मे तारा कामरूपनिवासिनी ।
आग्नेय्यां षोडशी पातु याम्यां धूमावती स्वयम् ॥१॥
verses.transliteration:
Om prācyāṃ rakṣatu me tārā kāmarūpanivāsinī |
āgneyyāṃ ṣoḍaśī pātu yāmyāṃ dhūmāvatī svayam ||1||
verses.translation: May Tārā, who dwells in Kāmarūpa, guard me in the east. In the southeast may Ṣoḍaśī (Tripurasundarī) protect me; in the south, Dhumāvatī herself.
verses.notes: Opening verse establishing directional protection framework

verses.sanskrit:
नैरृत्यां भैरवी पातु वारुण्यां भुवनेश्वरी ।
वायव्यां सततं पातु छिन्नमस्ता महेश्वरी ॥२॥
verses.transliteration:
nairṛtyāṃ bhairavī pātu vāruṇyāṃ bhuvaneśvarī |
vāyavyāṃ satataṃ pātu chinnamastā maheśvarī ||2||
verses.translation: In the southwest may Bhairavī protect; in the west, Bhuvaneśvarī. In the northwest may the great goddess Chinnamastā protect me always.

verses.sanskrit:
कौबेर्यां पातु मे देवी श्रीविद्या बगलामुखी ।
ऐशान्यां पातु मे नित्यं महात्रिपुरसुन्दरी ॥३॥
verses.transliteration:
kauberyāṃ pātu me devī śrīvidyā bagalāmukhī |
aiśānyāṃ pātu me nityaṃ mahātripurasundarī ||3||
verses.translation: In Kubera's quarter (the north) may the goddess Śrīvidyā—Bagalāmukhī—protect me. In Īśāna's quarter (the northeast) may Mahā‑Tripurasundarī protect me always.
verses.notes: Completes primary directional coverage with Śrīvidyā emphasis

verses.sanskrit:
ऊर्ध्वं रक्षतु मे विद्या मातङ्गीपीठवासिनी ।
सर्वतः पातु मे नित्यं कामाख्या कालिका स्वयम् ॥४॥
verses.transliteration:
ūrdhvaṃ rakṣatu me vidyā mātaṅgīpīṭhavāsinī |
sarvataḥ pātu me nityaṃ kāmākhyā kālikā svayam ||4||
verses.translation: Above, may Vidyā Mātangī, who abides at her sacred seat, guard me. On all sides may Kālikā of Kāmākhyā protect me always.
verses.notes: Establishes vertical and comprehensive protection; references Kāmākhyā pīṭha

verses.sanskrit:
ब्रह्मरूपा महाविद्या सर्वविद्यामयी स्वयम् ।
शीर्षे रक्षतु मे दुर्गा भालं श्रीभवगेहिनी ॥५॥
verses.transliteration:
brahmarūpā mahāvidyā sarvavidyāmayī svayam |
śīrṣe rakṣatu me durgā bhālaṃ śrībhavagehinī ||5||
verses.translation: She is the Great Vidyā, of the very form of Brahman, the essence of all knowledges. May Durgā guard my head; may Śrī‑Bhavagehinī guard my forehead.
verses.notes: Transitions to anatomical protection; establishes philosophical foundation

verses.sanskrit:
त्रिपुरा भ्रुयुगे पातु शर्वाणी पातु नासिकाम् ।
चक्षुषी चण्डिका पातु श्रोत्रे नीलसरस्वती ॥६॥
verses.transliteration:
tripurā bhruyuge pātu śarvāṇī pātu nāsikām |
cakṣuṣī caṇḍikā pātu śrotre nīlasarasvatī ||6||
verses.translation: May Tripurā guard my brows; may Śarvaṇī (Pārvatī) guard my nose. May Caṇḍikā guard my eyes; may Nīla‑Sarasvatī guard my ears.
verses.notes: Systematic protection of facial features and sensory organs

verses.sanskrit:
मुखं सौम्यमुखी पातु ग्रीवां रक्षतु पार्वती ।
जिह्वां रक्षतु मे देवी जिह्वाललनभीषणा ॥७॥
verses.transliteration:
mukhaṃ saumyamukhī pātu grīvāṃ rakṣatu pārvatī |
jihvāṃ rakṣatu me devī jihvālalanabhīṣaṇā ||7||
verses.translation: May the gentle‑faced One protect my face; may Pārvatī guard my neck. May the goddess—fearsome to foes—guard my tongue.
verses.notes: Protection of communication organs; contrasts gentle and fierce aspects

verses.sanskrit:
वाग्देवी वदनं पातु वक्षः पातु महेश्वरी ।
बाहू महाभुजा पातु कराङ्गुलीः सुरेश्वरी ॥८॥
verses.transliteration:
vāgdevī vadanaṃ pātu vakṣaḥ pātu maheśvarī |
bāhū mahābhujā pātu karāṅgulīḥ sureśvarī ||8||
verses.translation: May Vāgdevī protect my speech and mouth; may Maheśvarī guard my chest. May the Mighty‑Armed One protect my arms, and the Queen of the gods protect my fingers.
verses.notes: Protection of creative and active faculties; emphasis on divine power

verses.sanskrit:
पृष्ठतः पातु भीमास्या कट्यां देवी दिगम्बरी ।
उदरं पातु मे नित्यं महाविद्या महोदरी ॥९॥
verses.transliteration:
pṛṣṭhataḥ pātu bhīmāsyā kaṭyāṃ devī digambarī |
udaraṃ pātu me nityaṃ mahāvidyā mahodarī ||9||
verses.translation: From behind may Bhīmāsyā protect; at the hips, the goddess Digambarī. May the Great Vidyā Mahodari always protect my abdomen.
verses.notes: Protection of vital core areas; emphasis on fierce protective aspects

verses.sanskrit:
उग्रतारा महादेवी जङ्घोरू परिरक्षतु ।
उग्रतारा गुदं मुष्कं च मेढ्रं च नाभिं च सुरसुन्दरी ॥१०॥
verses.transliteration:
ugratārā mahādevī jaṅghorū parirakṣatu |
ugratārā gudaṃ muṣkaṃ ca meḍhraṃ ca nābhiṃ ca surasundarī ||10||
verses.translation: May the great goddess Ugratārā protect my shanks and thighs. May Ugratārā—Surasundarī—guard the anus, the scrotum, the phallus, and the navel.
verses.notes: Complete protection of lower body including reproductive organs

verses.sanskrit:
पादाङ्गुलीः सदा पातु भवानी त्रिदशेश्वरी ।
रक्तमांसास्थिमज्जादीन् पातु देवी शवासना ॥११॥
verses.transliteration:
pādāṅgulīḥ sadā pātu bhavānī tridaśeśvarī |
raktamāṃsāsthimajjādīn pātu devī śavāsanā ||11||
verses.translation: May Bhavānī, queen of the gods, always protect my toes. May the goddess who is seated upon a corpse protect my blood, flesh, bones, marrow, and the rest.
verses.notes: Completes anatomical coverage; includes internal bodily substances

verses.sanskrit:
महाभयेषु घोरेषु महाभयनिवारिणी ।
पातु देवी महामाया कामाख्यापीठवासिनी ॥१२॥
verses.transliteration:
mahābhayeṣu ghoreṣu mahābhayanivāriṇī |
pātu devī mahāmāyā kāmākhyāpīṭhavāsinī ||12||
verses.translation: In great and terrible dangers, may the remover of great fear—Mahāmāyā, who dwells at the seat of Kāmākhyā—protect me.
verses.notes: Invocation for extreme circumstances; references primary Śakti pīṭha

verses.sanskrit:
भस्माचलगता दिव्यसिंहासनकृताश्रया ।
पातु श्रीकालिकादेवी सर्वोत्पातेषु सर्वदा ॥१३॥
verses.transliteration:
bhasmācalagatā divyasiṃhāsanakṛtāśrayā |
pātu śrīkālikādevī sarvotpāteṣu sarvadā ||13||
verses.translation: Residing on the mountain of ash, enthroned upon the divine lion seat—may Śrī Kālika‑devī protect me from every calamity, always.
verses.notes: Cosmic imagery; universal protection from all forms of calamity

verses.sanskrit:
रक्षाहीनं तु यत्स्थानं कवचेनापि वर्जितम् ।
तत्सर्वं सर्वदा पातु सर्वरक्षणकारिणी ॥१४॥
verses.transliteration:
rakṣāhīnaṃ tu yatsthānaṃ kavacenāpi varjitam |
tatsarvaṃ sarvadā pātu sarvarakṣaṇakāriṇī ||14||
verses.translation: And whatever place is left unprotected—omitted even by this armor—may the All‑Protecting Goddess guard all of that, at all times.
verses.notes: Concluding verse ensuring complete protection; acknowledges potential omissions
